title: Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally
tagline: Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally — spiritual town of Kerala

heroImage.alt: Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally, Kerala

overview.short: Mar Thoma Sleeha Syro-Malabar Church is a pilgrimage church located in Thulappally, Syro-Malabar Catholic Eparchy of Kanjirappally in the Indian state of Kerala.
overview.description: Mar Thoma Sleeha Syro-Malabar Church is a pilgrimage church located in Thulappally, Syro-Malabar Catholic Eparchy of Kanjirappally in the Indian state of Kerala. The St. Thomas's feast festivities held there every July attract a number of devotees from in and around Kerala.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Edakadathy
topPlaces.description: Edakadathy is a village located near Mukkoottuthara in Erumely gram panchayat, Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It is situated near the border of Kottayam district and Pathanamthitta district, but officially recorded as a part of the former.

topPlaces.name: Thulappally
topPlaces.description: Thulappally is a small village in Perunad panchayath of Ranni tehsil,Thiruvalla Revenue Division in Pathanamthitta district of Kerala state, India.

topPlaces.name: Kanamala
topPlaces.description: Kanamala (), commonly known by the name Pambavalley, is a village in Erumely panchayath in Kanjirappally tehsil of Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is situated in the border of Kottayam and Pathanamthitta districts, but officially recorded as a part of Kottayam district only.

topPlaces.name: Kanamala Bridge
topPlaces.description: Kanamala Bridge is a concrete bridge in Kanamala, Kerala that connects Kottayam and Pathanamthitta districts through Pamba River. The bridge was opened for public on 23 December 2014.

topPlaces.name: Koruthodu
topPlaces.description: Koruthodu is a village/town in Kanjirappally Taluk, Kottayam District of Kerala state, India. It is situated on the Highway connecting Mundakayam to Sabarimala temple, Kerala, and is at the border of Pathanamthitta, Kottayam and Idukki districts.

topPlaces.name: Nilakkal
topPlaces.description: Nilakkal, also spelled Nilackal, is an important base camp of Sabarimala Hindu pilgrims located in Ranni tehsil, in Thiruvalla Revenue Division of Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala. Sabarimala temple is located at a distance of 23 km (14 mi) from Nilakkal.

topPlaces.name: Plappally
topPlaces.description: Plappally is a settlement located in the Goodrical range of the Ranni Forest Division in the state of Kerala, India. The place is an intersection point of Mannarakulanji–Chalakkayam state highway (NH-183) and Erumely–Thulappally–Plappally route.

topPlaces.name: Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple located in Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala. The temple which is dedicated to Lord Shiva is administered by Travancore Devaswom Board.

items.note: Edakadathy is a village located near Mukkoottuthara in Erumely gram panchayat, Kottayam district, Kerala, India. It is situated near the border of Kottayam district and Pathanamthitta district, but officially recorded as a part of the former.
Thulappally is a small village in Perunad panchayath of Ranni tehsil,Thiruvalla Revenue Division in Pathanamthitta district of Kerala state, India.

Kanamala (), commonly known by the name Pambavalley, is a village in Erumely panchayath in Kanjirappally tehsil of Kottayam district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is situated in the border of Kottayam and Pathanamthitta districts, but officially recorded as a part of Kottayam district only.
Kanamala Bridge is a concrete bridge in Kanamala, Kerala that connects Kottayam and Pathanamthitta districts through Pamba River. The bridge was opened for public on 23 December 2014.

Koruthodu is a village/town in Kanjirappally Taluk, Kottayam District of Kerala state, India. It is situated on the Highway connecting Mundakayam to Sabarimala temple, Kerala, and is at the border of Pathanamthitta, Kottayam and Idukki districts.

Nilakkal, also spelled Nilackal, is an important base camp of Sabarimala Hindu pilgrims located in Ranni tehsil, in Thiruvalla Revenue Division of Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala. Sabarimala temple is located at a distance of 23 km (14 mi) from Nilakkal.

Plappally is a settlement located in the Goodrical range of the Ranni Forest Division in the state of Kerala, India. The place is an intersection point of Mannarakulanji–Chalakkayam state highway (NH-183) and Erumely–Thulappally–Plappally route.

Nilakkal Sree Mahadeva Temple is a Hindu temple located in Pathanamthitta district in the Indian state of Kerala. The temple which is dedicated to Lord Shiva is administered by Travancore Devaswom Board.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (102 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (96 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally?
faq.a: Stays in Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mar Thoma Sleeha Church Thulappally?
faq.a: The top attractions are Edakadathy, Thulappally, Kanamala, Kanamala Bridge, Koruthodu.
